paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: SUBJECT: Exercise STEEL HORIZON 26-3 Operations Brief — Draft v0.4

paragraphs.rationale: Information identifying critical assets, vulnerabilities, indicators, or sensitive activities under DoDD 5205.02E.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 1. PURPOSE. This brief outlines the concept of operations for Exercise STEEL HORIZON 26-3, a III MEF combined-arms rehearsal scheduled vicinity Camp Pendleton 12-19 May 2026. Audience: III MEF G-3, MARFORPAC plans, and coalition liaison officers from Australia (AUS) and the United Kingdom (GBR).

paragraphs.rationale: Information identifying critical assets and sensitive activities under DoDD 5205.02E.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 2. PARTICIPATING UNITS. 1st Marine Division (-) (Reinforced) with attachments from 1st MAW and 1st MLG. Coalition contingent: 3 RAR (AUS, 88 personnel) and 42 Commando (GBR, 64 personnel). Notional opposing force is portrayed by 1st Bn 4th Marines acting as a near-peer mechanized infantry battalion.

paragraphs.rationale: Information identifying critical assets and vulnerabilities under DoDD 5205.02E.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 3. FORCE PROTECTION POSTURE. FPCON BRAVO will be in effect throughout the exercise. The vulnerability assessment identifies the western motor pool gate and the unmarked range entry on Old Stage Road as the two highest-risk ingress points. Manning roster for the entry control points is at Annex C; rotation schedule is attached at Annex D and is not to be released outside the III MEF FP cell.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 4. INTELLIGENCE SUPPORT. 1st Intelligence Battalion will provide a tailored intel package built from open-source reporting and previously-released exercise products. No classified intelligence is required for this rehearsal. The G-2 estimate for the OPFOR cell uses publicly-available platform data on the BMP-3 and T-72B3 for vehicle role-play.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 5. LOGISTICS. Class I, III, and V supply will be drawn from MCLB Barstow against existing exercise allocations. Combat Service Support Detachment 11 will operate the forward distribution point. There are no proprietary contractor rate cards or vendor cost build-ups in this brief; all support is organic.

paragraphs.rationale: Information identifying critical assets, vulnerabilities, indicators, or sensitive activities under DoDD 5205.02E.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 6. RELEASABILITY INTENT. The author requests this brief be marked at the lowest level consistent with operational security, with appropriate caveat to permit coalition LNO read-in. Final marking is at the discretion of the III MEF G-2 OCA-equivalent reviewer.

doc_brief.executive_brief: The recommended overall marking for the Exercise STEEL HORIZON 26-3 Operations Brief is CUI//SP-OPSEC due to the presence of information that identifies critical assets and vulnerabilities, as outlined in multiple paragraphs of the document. This marking balances the need for operational security with the intent to share relevant information with coalition partners, specifically Australia and the United Kingdom. The document should be marked as releasable to the USA and Five Eyes (FVEY) partners to facilitate collaboration while maintaining necessary security protocols.
doc_brief.over_marking_risk: Over-marking the document could hinder timely information sharing with coalition partners, potentially impacting operational effectiveness.
doc_brief.under_marking_risk: Under-marking poses a significant risk of compromising sensitive operational details, which could lead to security breaches or spillage of critical information.
doc_brief.ipo_recommendation: Approve the document with the marking CUI//SP-OPSEC and the releasability designation REL TO USA, FVEY.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: SUBJECT: III MEF G-2 Weekly Pacific INTSUM 26-W17 (Draft)

paragraphs.rationale: Information identifying critical assets, vulnerabilities, indicators, or sensitive activities under DoDD 5205.02E.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY. This summary covers the period 20-26 April 2026 and consolidates open-source and previously-released finished intelligence on PRC PLAN surface activity in the South China Sea and Philippine Sea. No new collection is reported.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 2. PLAN SURFACE ACTIVITY. Two Type 055 Renhai-class cruisers were observed transiting the Bashi Channel southbound on 22 April 2026 per commercial AIS and open-source SAR overlays. This pattern is consistent with the routine quarterly task-group rotation and is not assessed as a precursor to escalation.

paragraphs.rationale: Information identifying critical assets, vulnerabilities, indicators, or sensitive activities under DoDD 5205.02E.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 3. COALITION DISCLOSURE NOTE. Source-derived assessments in paragraph 4 are tagged as not releasable to foreign nationals under existing III MEF G-2 disclosure guidance. Paragraphs 1-3 may be shared with FVEY partners with appropriate marking.

paragraphs.rationale: Information identifying critical assets, vulnerabilities, indicators, or sensitive activities under DoDD 5205.02E.
trigger_phrases: not to be released to foreign nationals
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 4. FINISHED ASSESSMENT — U.S. ONLY. Internal G-2 assessment of PLAN tactical employment patterns derived from previously-released MIL-DEC products. Detailed indicators and warning thresholds are referenced internally only and are not to be released to foreign nationals. NOFORN handling is required for this paragraph as written.

paragraphs.rationale: Information identifying critical assets, vulnerabilities, indicators, or sensitive activities under DoDD 5205.02E.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 5. FORCE PROTECTION INDICATORS. No new force protection indicators against U.S. forces in the III MEF AOR were identified during the reporting period. FPCON BRAVO remains in effect across all III MEF installations.

paragraphs.rationale: Contains Personally Identifiable Information (PII) protected under the Privacy Act of 1974.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 6. PERSONNEL DATA. Two named G-2 personnel are referenced in the source-handling annex with full names, EDIPI numbers, and home-station phone extensions for chain-of-custody purposes. These personnel data fields must not appear in any externally-distributed copy.

paragraphs.rationale: The paragraph contains personnel data, which is considered Personally Identifiable Information (PII) protected under the Privacy Act.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 7. RECOMMENDED MARKING DISPOSITION. The G-2 author requests review for split marking — body of report at one level, Annex A (personnel data) at another — to maximize coalition shareability without compromising privacy obligations.

doc_brief.executive_brief: The document contains sensitive information that necessitates a CUI marking, particularly due to the presence of Personally Identifiable Information (PII) in the personnel data section. While some sections may be suitable for sharing with FVEY partners, the overall sensitivity of the content, especially regarding force protection and operational security, warrants a NOFORN designation. This ensures that critical assessments and personnel data are adequately protected from unauthorized foreign access.
doc_brief.over_marking_risk: Over-marking could hinder timely information sharing with coalition partners, potentially slowing down collaborative efforts.
doc_brief.under_marking_risk: Under-marking poses a significant risk of compromising sensitive operational details and personal data, leading to potential spillage and security breaches.
doc_brief.ipo_recommendation: Approve the document with the recommended CUI//SP-PRVCY marking and NOFORN handling to safeguard sensitive information.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: SUBJECT: 2d Marine Logistics Group Annual CUI Refresher Training Memorandum

paragraphs.rationale: Information requiring protection against public disclosure under FOIA Exemption 2-9.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 1. PURPOSE. This memorandum directs all 2d MLG personnel — military, civilian, and embedded contractor — to complete Annual CUI Refresher Training NLT 30 June 2026 in accordance with DoDM 5200.01 Vol 2 and 32 CFR Part 2002.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 2. APPLICABILITY. All personnel assigned, attached, or providing direct support to 2d MLG. Contractor personnel will complete training under the data-handling clauses of their contract and will provide the contracting officer's representative with a completion certificate.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 3. TRAINING CONTENT. The training module covers: (a) the CUI program lifecycle, (b) lawful-government-purpose disclosure standards, (c) the difference between CUI Basic and CUI Specified, (d) marking mechanics including banner lines and portion marks, and (e) destruction standards under NIST SP 800-88. None of this content is itself controlled.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 4. ADMINISTRATIVE NOTES. The training is hosted on the unclassified MarineNet learning management system. There are no PII fields, no force-protection indicators, no proprietary contractor data, and no operational details in this memorandum. The memorandum is intended for unrestricted internal distribution and posting on the 2d MLG SharePoint announcements page.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 5. POINT OF CONTACT. Direct questions to the 2d MLG Information Protection Officer through the chain of command. The IPO mailbox is published on the public 2d MLG portal.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 6. DISTRIBUTION. Unrestricted internal distribution. May be reposted on the public 2d MLG website if no banner-line marking is applied at the page level.

doc_brief.executive_brief: The document is a memorandum directing personnel of the 2d Marine Logistics Group to complete Annual CUI Refresher Training. It contains no sensitive information that requires protection and is intended for unrestricted internal distribution. Given the nature of the content, it is appropriate to mark the document as UNCLASSIFIED, allowing for broader dissemination while ensuring compliance with DoDM 5200.01 guidelines.
doc_brief.over_marking_risk: Over-marking this document could hinder effective communication and sharing of information within the coalition.
doc_brief.under_marking_risk: Under-marking could lead to unintentional disclosure of sensitive information, resulting in potential compromise or spillage.
doc_brief.ipo_recommendation: Mark the document as UNCLASSIFIED and approve for REL TO USA, FVEY distribution.

paragraphs.rationale: Public-releasable information; no CUI categories triggered.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: SUBJECT: Statement of Work — Predictive Maintenance Pilot, AAV-P7/A1 Hub Bearings (Draft v0.2)

paragraphs.rationale: Source-selection / procurement-sensitive information under FAR 3.104.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 1. SCOPE. Vendor shall provide a 90-day predictive-maintenance pilot for AAV-P7/A1 hub bearings at MCLB Albany. Deliverables include vibration-feature extraction software, a trained classifier, and a written final report. This SOW is pre-award and contains source-selection-sensitive content.

paragraphs.rationale: Source-selection / procurement-sensitive information under FAR 3.104.
trigger_phrases: Disclosure outside the source-selection evaluation board would prejudice the competitive process.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 2. PRICING. Vendor's labor rate is $187.50 per hour fully burdened for senior data engineers and $142.00 per hour for mid-level engineers. Vendor's proposed Other Direct Costs (ODC) include $48,200 for sensor instrumentation and $11,400 for travel. These rates are vendor-proprietary and were submitted under the company's Best and Final Offer (BAFO) of 14 April 2026. Disclosure outside the source-selection evaluation board would prejudice the competitive process.

paragraphs.rationale: Information subject to ITAR export-control restrictions.
trigger_phrases: may not be transferred to foreign nationals
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 3. TECHNICAL DATA. The pilot will produce vibration spectrograms and a trained classifier model. The model and any derived technical data package (TDP) for the AAV-P7/A1 drive train are subject to ITAR controls under USML Category VII (Ground Vehicles) and may not be transferred to foreign nationals or released outside the United States without an approved export license. Source code shipped under this SOW is deemed-export controlled.

paragraphs.rationale: Information contains Personally Identifiable Information (PII) protected under the Privacy Act of 1974.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 4. PERSONNEL. Vendor will identify three named engineers for the pilot. Their full names, work email addresses, dates of birth, and EDIPI-equivalent contractor identifiers will be provided to the contracting officer's representative under separate cover. These fields are protected under the Privacy Act and shall not appear in any deliverable distributed beyond the COR.

paragraphs.rationale: Information requiring protection against public disclosure under FOIA Exemption 2-9.
trigger_phrases: not for release outside the MCLB Albany Information Protection Office
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 5. PERFORMANCE LOCATION. Work is performed on-site at MCLB Albany Building 3700, the Albany Predictive Maintenance Lab. The lab's physical security plan, badging procedures, and after-hours access roster are referenced in Annex B and are not for release outside the MCLB Albany Information Protection Office.

paragraphs.rationale: Source-selection / procurement-sensitive information under FAR 3.104.
paragraphs.paragraph_text: 6. RELEASABILITY. Vendor acknowledges that source-selection-sensitive material, ITAR-controlled technical data, and Privacy-Act-protected personnel data each carry distinct CUI handling requirements. Vendor shall mark all deliverables in accordance with DoDM 5200.01 Vol 2 and the contract Data Item Descriptions (DIDs).

doc_brief.executive_brief: The document contains multiple categories of Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI), primarily focused on source-selection-sensitive procurement information, ITAR-controlled technical data, and Privacy Act-protected personnel data. Given the sensitive nature of the information, particularly in relation to competitive procurement and export controls, the recommended overall marking is CUI//SP-PROCURE. This marking ensures compliance with DoDM 5200.01 and protects against unauthorized disclosure while facilitating necessary internal sharing.
doc_brief.over_marking_risk: Over-marking could hinder timely information sharing among coalition partners and slow down the procurement process.
doc_brief.under_marking_risk: Under-marking poses a significant risk of compromising sensitive procurement data and could lead to unauthorized disclosures of PII and export-controlled information.
doc_brief.ipo_recommendation: Approve the document with the recommended marking of CUI//SP-PROCURE and a releasability call of NOFORN.
